claim_policy.rule: Risk signals are not treated as final target claims without authoritative source evidence, identifier confidence and scope proof.
claim_policy.memo_language: Use source, source_status, checked_at, freshness, fallback_used, confidence, limitation and diligence question for every memo claim.

cyber_infrastructure.title: Cyber infrastructure evidence
cyber_infrastructure.summary: 2 infrastructure item(s) are classified as third-party edge/provider context, not target-owned exposure.
cyber_infrastructure.source: Cloudflare DoH + Shodan InternetDB + IANA/RIR RDAP

rows.claim: 35.71.142.77 resolves through Amazon Web Services with ports 80, 443; no CVE signal returned.
rows.limitation: Provider/CDN ports are not target-owned exposure without additional asset proof.
rows.diligence_question: Confirm CDN/WAF/hosting provider scope and whether any origin servers bypass this edge layer.

rows.claim: 52.223.52.2 resolves through Amazon Web Services with ports 80, 443; no CVE signal returned.
rows.limitation: Provider/CDN ports are not target-owned exposure without additional asset proof.
rows.diligence_question: Confirm CDN/WAF/hosting provider scope and whether any origin servers bypass this edge layer.

diligence_questions: Confirm CDN/WAF/hosting provider scope and whether any origin servers bypass this edge layer.

sanctions_screening.title: Sanctions and PEP screening evidence
sanctions_screening.summary: No claim-safe sanctions/PEP match was found for bunq B.V. via sanctions_enricher_v1.

rows.claim: No claim-safe sanctions or PEP risk match was found for bunq B.V..
rows.limitation: Screening is point-in-time and depends on available identifiers, transliteration and dataset freshness.
rows.diligence_question: Confirm legal name, registration number, UBOs and directors before final compliance sign-off.

diligence_questions: Confirm legal name, registration number, UBOs and directors before final compliance sign-off.
Are UBOs, directors and material related parties also in scope for sanctions/PEP screening?

finance_forensics.title: Finance forensic evidence
finance_forensics.summary: Finance forensic evidence for bunq B.V. records source unavailability while the Benford analyzer route itself is verified only as a technical path.
finance_forensics.source: DueSight Financial Bridge + Benford Analyzer + DE Register Filing Gate

rows.claim: No memo-safe official filing or jaarrekening source data is available for bunq B.V. in this run.
rows.limitation: Source route needs recovery or primary provider evidence before production use.
rows.diligence_question: Collect official jaarrekening/XBRL output or seller P&L export before relying on finance findings.

rows.claim: Benford analyzer route completed for bunq B.V. on a deterministic smoke population. Only 0 target-specific finance values were extractable, below the 30-value minimum. Route-smoke metrics: chi2=0.0282, MAD=0.0142, n=64, deviation=LOW.
rows.limitation: This verifies that the Benford analyzer path works; it is not a target-specific finance finding and cannot support a memo conclusion.
rows.diligence_question: Collect an official filing, XBRL extract, P&L export or transaction-level population before using Benford as target evidence.

diligence_questions: Collect official jaarrekening/XBRL output or seller P&L export before relying on finance findings.
Collect an official filing, XBRL extract, P&L export or transaction-level population before using Benford as target evidence.

ownership_register.title: Ownership and register evidence
ownership_register.summary: Ownership/register evidence for bunq B.V. found candidate context that needs official register confirmation.

rows.claim: Public register route produced an identity candidate for bunq B.V.: bunq B.V. (KvK 54992060). Match confidence 0.96.
rows.limitation: Public or corroborative register output is review-only until confirmed with the official KvK Basisprofiel/Dataservice response.
rows.diligence_question: Which official register extract and legal identifiers prove the target entity before relying on ownership findings?

rows.claim: Ownership proxy built partial ownership-tree context for bunq B.V..
rows.source: DueSight Algorithmic UBO Proxy / OpenKvK, GLEIF Level 2
rows.limitation: Partial tree context is not a complete UBO conclusion and must be reconciled against official/control documents.
rows.diligence_question: Which ownership links are unsupported, stale or missing percentages in the reconstructed tree?

diligence_questions: Which official register extract and legal identifiers prove the target entity before relying on ownership findings?
Which ownership links are unsupported, stale or missing percentages in the reconstructed tree?

rows.limitation: Registration context is not a no-risk conclusion; scope, license class and exact legal entity must be verified.

reputation_adverse_media.title: Reputation and adverse media evidence
reputation_adverse_media.summary: Reputation evidence for bunq B.V. includes target-relevant adverse-media signals requiring source review.

rows.claim: Adverse-media route found 1 target-relevant article signal(s) for bunq B.V.. Categories: legal. Example title: Fine for bunq B.V. for insufficient customer due diligence - dnb.nl.
rows.source: DueSight adverse-media source ladder (public news + legal metadata)
rows.limitation: Adverse media is a triage signal unless confirmed against authoritative sources, exact entity identifiers and publication context.

diligence_questions: Which article, regulator/court source, publication date and entity identifiers make the adverse-media signal deal-relevant?

rows.claim: Adverse-media route found 1 target-relevant article signal(s) for bunq B.V.. Categories: legal. Example title: Fine for bunq B.V. for insufficient customer due diligence - dnb.nl.

rows.diligence_question: Which article, regulator/court source, publication date and entity identifiers make the adverse-media signal deal-relevant?

diligence_questions: Confirm CDN/WAF/hosting provider scope and whether any origin servers bypass this edge layer.
Confirm legal name, registration number, UBOs and directors before final compliance sign-off.
Are UBOs, directors and material related parties also in scope for sanctions/PEP screening?
Collect official jaarrekening/XBRL output or seller P&L export before relying on finance findings.
Collect an official filing, XBRL extract, P&L export or transaction-level population before using Benford as target evidence.
Which official register extract and legal identifiers prove the target entity before relying on ownership findings?
Which ownership links are unsupported, stale or missing percentages in the reconstructed tree?
Which license scope, regulated activity and legal entity does the registration cover?
Which article, regulator/court source, publication date and entity identifiers make the adverse-media signal deal-relevant?
Which profile claims are official-source confirmed versus inferred from search/news?
